expectations.text: Flags MFA scope gap against IA.L2-3.5.3 (MFA required for local and network access to privileged accounts and network access to non-privileged accounts — cloud email alone is insufficient)
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'IA.L2-3.5.3 | IA | MFA for local/network access to privileged accounts and network access to non-privileged accounts | NOT MET... MFA only covers cloud email. VPN, on-prem AD, CAD workstations, file shares, and any privileged accounts elsewhere are not covered.'

expectations.text: Flags FIPS-validated cryptography gap (SC.L2-3.13.11 requires FIPS-validated cryptography to protect CUI confidentiality) and the flat-network/shared-drive state against scoping and SC.L2-3.13.1/AC.L2-3.1.3 (boundary protection / control CUI flow), recommending segmentation or an enclave
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'SC.L2-3.13.11... Employ FIPS-validated cryptography to protect CUI | NOT MET... No FIPS-validated encryption'. Separately: 'AC.L2-3.1.3 | AC | Control the flow of CUI... NOT MET... Flat network with CUI on shared drives = no CUI flow control, no segmentation.' Remediation Tier 1 #1 recommends segmenting the network and moving CUI into 'an access-controlled, ideally isolated enclave.'

expectations.text: Flags untested IR plan against IR.L2-3.6.3 (test incident response capability) and the stale SSP against CA.L2-3.12.4 (SSP must be current)
expectations.evidence: 'IR.L2-3.6.3 | IR | Test the organizational incident response capability | NOT MET... Plan "never tested" — explicit gap.' And 'CA.L2-3.12.4 | CA | Develop, document, and periodically update SSPs | NOT MET... SSP last updated in 2022 — four years stale.'

expectations.text: Correctly credits compliant items (awareness training AT.L2-3.2.1/.2; personnel screening PS.L2-3.9.1) instead of marking everything deficient
expectations.evidence: 'AT.L2-3.2.1 / 3.2.2 / 3.2.3... MET (general)...' and 'PS.L2-3.9.1... Screen individuals prior to authorizing CUI access | MET... Background screening for all staff — meets this practice as stated.'

expectations.text: Explains SPRS scoring impact using the DoD methodology: score starts from 110 with weighted deductions (5/3/1 points; MFA and FIPS crypto are 5-point/partial-credit items), and prioritises remediation by point value and POA&M eligibility
expectations.evidence: 'Starting from a maximum score of 110... IA.L2-3.5.3 (MFA) | 5 pts | −5', 'SC.L2-3.13.11 (FIPS crypto) | 5 pts | −5', 'AC.L2-3.1.3... | 5 pts | −5', plus 1-point items. Remediation is organized into 'Tier 1 — Critical practices... (no POA&M eligibility)' and 'Tier 2 — High-value, POA&M-eligible', explicitly tying prioritization to both point value and POA&M eligibility.
